note: January at Ambaji: 12-24°C, crisp hilltop air and dry darshan days. Winter works well for the Shakti Peeth climb, though Bhadarvi Poonam in Sept is the true peak.
January at Bhavnagar: 14-28°C, dry Gulf breeze and clear skies. Solid window for Takhteshwar and Palitana day trips — still pleasant before February warmth.
January at Chinnar: 18-30°C, rain-shadow sanctuary stays dry and trails hold firm. Grizzled squirrels visible — good window though Feb-Mar is the sharpest.
January at Coimbatore: 18-30°C, dry and breezy — comfortable for Adiyogi visits and Nilgiri gateway stops. Peak is December, but January still works.
January at Courtallam: 20-30°C, falls flow modestly after retreating monsoon. Pleasant bathing window — true peak is Aadi season (Jul-Aug).
January at Dakor: 12-26°C, cool mornings and dry temple days. Solid darshan conditions — Holi and Sharad Poonam draw bigger crowds later.
January at Eravikulam: 8-20°C, clear high-altitude days and tahr visible on Rajamalai slopes. Good window — April rhododendron bloom edges ahead for photography.
January at Gandhinagar: 10-26°C, cool mornings and tree-lined boulevards at their softest. Comfortable for Akshardham and Adalaj — peak crispness held over from December.
January at Meghamalai: 12-22°C, dry cloud-forest days with clear viewpoints. Estate roads workable — book stays in advance given the tiny footprint.
January at Rajkot: 12-27°C, dry Kathiawadi afternoons and cool evenings. Solid window for Gandhi heritage circuit and the food trail — peak is December.
January at Saputara: 10-24°C, dry misty mornings on Gujarat's only hill station. Pleasant but quieter — the winter festival extends the post-peak buzz.
January at Silent Valley: 15-26°C, dry rainforest trails and clear canopy walks. Good visibility — permits steady, though Feb-Mar sharpens wildlife sightings.
January at Surat: 14-29°C, coolest window of the year and prime street-food weather. Works well — peak crispness held from December before heat returns.
January at Thrissur: 22-32°C, dry pre-Pooram lull with temples open dawn to dusk. Solid cultural window — April Pooram is the showpiece.
January at Vadodara: 12-28°C, cool palace-garden mornings and dry museum days. Pleasant window — peak is December, but January still comfortable for walking.
January at Valparai: 14-24°C, dry hairpin drives and clear tea-estate views. Macaque sightings steady — good window before pre-summer haze sets in.
February at Ambaji: 14-28°C, dry hilltop air before heat builds. Works well for Shakti Peeth darshan — shoulder before Gujarat's summer burns in.
February at Anamalai: 16-28°C, dry Topslip trails and macaque sightings steady. Solid safari window — forest dept jeeps run, though Mar-May see denser wildlife visibility.
February at Bhavnagar: 16-30°C, dry Gulf breeze and pleasant palace walks. Edge of peak — comfortable before March heat picks up.
February at Champaner-Pavagadh: 14-30°C, dry archaeological trails and clear ridgeline climbs. Works well — ropeway and Pavagadh steps both comfortable.
February at Chinnar: 18-31°C, driest sanctuary days and firm trails. Solid birding window — rain-shadow forest at its most accessible.
February at Coimbatore: 20-32°C, dry and warming — still pleasant for Adiyogi and Nilgiri gateway stops. Shoulder window before March heat.
February at Dakor: 14-30°C, dry temple days and manageable crowds. Pleasant window — Holi and monsoon festivals draw heavier turnout.
February at Dharmasthala: 18-32°C, dry coastal-belt days and temple open dawn to dusk. Annadana runs as ever — solid window before pre-monsoon humidity.
February at Dholavira: 14-30°C, dry Harappan ruins and clear Rann light. Works well — heat climbs fast after February, so this edge-of-peak matters.
February at Dwarka: 18-30°C, dry coastal air and steady sea breeze for temple visits. Solid Char Dham window — April-May bring fierce heat.
February at Gandhinagar: 14-30°C, dry tree-lined days and soft morning light. Pleasant — shoulder before March heat erodes outdoor comfort.
February at Girnar: 14-29°C, dry 10,000-step climb conditions and clear summit views. Solid trekking window — heat escalates quickly by March.
February at Idukki: 18-30°C, dry cardamom hills and steady dam views. Works well for wildlife spotting — peak cool has passed but still comfortable.
February at Junagadh: 14-30°C, dry fort climbs and clear Ashokan edict visits. Solid heritage window — edge of peak before Girnar region warms.
February at Kukke Subramanya: 18-32°C, dry Western Ghats temple days and Kumara Parvatha trailhead firm. Works well before pre-monsoon humidity rises.
February at Kutch: 15-30°C, dry Rann edge and workable handicraft village drives. Solid window — Rann Utsav tail end, crowds thinning.
February at Lonar: 14-30°C, dry crater rim walks and clear geological visibility. Pleasant window before Vidarbha heat — trails firm.
February at Lothal: 14-30°C, dry ruins and comfortable museum visits. Works well — edge of peak before the Gujarat summer erodes outdoor time.
February at Margao: 20-32°C, dry market mornings and Konkani-food weather. Solid South Goa window — shoulder to peak, crowds thinner than Christmas rush.
February at Meghamalai: 12-24°C, dry cloud-forest days and clearer views than the monsoon months. Works well — estate booking still advance-only.
February at Modhera: 14-30°C, dry Sun Temple days and sharp carving light. Pleasant window — pre-equinox, before March heat.
February at Mudumalai: 18-32°C, dry Nilgiri foothill safaris and steady elephant sightings. Solid window — forest dept safaris run, pre-fire-season visibility still good.
February at Munnar: 12-25°C, dry tea-estate days and clear peak views. Solid window — shoulder to peak, crowds thinner than December holiday rush.
February at Nelliyampathy: 14-26°C, dry plantation days and quiet trails. Works well — minimal infrastructure means pre-book stays.
February at Palitana: 14-30°C, dry sacred climb conditions with 863 temples accessible. Solid pilgrimage window — heat rises fast by March.
February at Point Calimere: 20-30°C, flamingo flocks still holding and dry wetland trails. Solid birding — migratory numbers taper by March.
February at Ponda: 20-32°C, dry plantation walks and clear spice-trail visibility. Works well — shoulder to peak before pre-monsoon humidity builds.
February at Ponmudi: 18-28°C, dry hairpin drives and steady butterfly sightings. Pleasant window — 22 bends manageable before summer haze.
February at Porbandar: 16-29°C, dry coastal air and pleasant Gandhi heritage walks. Solid window — edge of peak before Saurashtra heat builds.
February at Rajkot: 14-30°C, dry and comfortable for Kathiawadi food trails. Works well — shoulder before March heat erodes street-food outings.
February at Rani ki Vav: 14-30°C, dry stepwell descents and sharp carving light. Solid window — shade in the seven levels holds even as days warm.
February at Saputara: 12-26°C, dry misty mornings and clear Western Ghats views. Works well — quieter than peak monsoon-season arrivals.
February at Shirdi: 16-32°C, dry temple-town days with 40,000 daily pilgrims steady. Solid darshan window before March heat thins queues less comfortably.
February at Silent Valley: 15-28°C, dry rainforest trails and clear canopy visibility. Works well — permit-gated, so numbers stay low regardless.
February at Somnath: 18-30°C, dry coastal Jyotirlinga darshan and clear Arabian Sea sunsets. Solid window — heat escalates by April.
February at Srirangapatna: 18-31°C, dry Kaveri island walks and comfortable fort tours. Works well — Tipu heritage circuit in pleasant light.
February at Statue of Unity: 14-30°C, dry Narmada valley views and clear viewing gallery visibility. Solid window before April heat erodes outdoor comfort.
February at Surat: 16-31°C, dry street-food evenings and pleasant diamond-market walks. Edge of peak — comfortable before March heat.
February at Thekkady: 18-28°C, dry Periyar Lake boat safaris and clear spice-plantation walks. Solid wildlife window — elephants visible at waterholes.
February at Thrissur: 22-33°C, dry cultural-capital days and temples open dawn to dusk. Works well — pre-Pooram lull, crowds thinner than April peak.
February at Trimbakeshwar: 14-30°C, dry Godavari source visits and comfortable Jyotirlinga darshan. Solid window before Maharashtra summer burns in.
February at Vadodara: 14-30°C, dry palace-garden tours and clear museum days. Pleasant window — shoulder before March heat.
February at Vagamon: 16-28°C, dry pine-forest walks and clear meadow paragliding windows. Works well — Kerala's underrated hills at calm.
February at Valparai: 15-26°C, dry hairpin drives and clear tea-estate views. Solid window — edge of peak before pre-summer haze.
February at Vedanthangal: 20-32°C, waterbird colonies still active and dry sanctuary trails. Works well — Chennai day-trip friendly before April heat.
February at Wayanad: 18-30°C, dry cave trails and clear plantation views. Solid window — heart-shaped lake accessible, shoulder to pre-monsoon.
April at Eravikulam: 10-22°C, pre-monsoon clarity and rhododendron hints on the high slopes. Works well — peak of accessibility before July closures.
June at Anamalai: 18-26°C, monsoon onset greens the Topslip forests and bamboo creeks. Works well but tiger-safari visibility is sharper in dry season.
June at Athirapally: 22-29°C, monsoon swells India's largest waterfall to full volume. Solid window — roads can be slippery, but the flow is at full force.
June at Courtallam: 22-30°C, monsoon refills the nine herb-infused falls. Works well for therapeutic bathing — true peak is Aadi (Jul-Aug).
June at Mudumalai: 20-29°C, monsoon greens the Nilgiri biosphere and elephants emerge. Safaris run but visibility varies — core dry-season peak is later.
July at Hogenakkal: 24-30°C, Kaveri swells and coracle rides run through thundering falls. Solid window — core monsoon flow, spray heavy, check ferry advisories.
August at Hogenakkal: 24-30°C, sustained monsoon keeps the falls at full volume and coracles running. Works well — 2,000-year oil-massage tradition stays active.
September at Coonoor: 14-24°C, monsoon retreating and tea slopes lush at 1,850m. Solid window — quieter than Ooty, toy train running pleasantly.
September at Eravikulam: 12-22°C, late monsoon clearing on tahr slopes. Park reopens mid-month post-calving — still misty, clearer windows week on week.
September at Idukki: 18-28°C, cardamom hills at their greenest as monsoon eases. Works well — dam views clearing, wildlife spotting improves through the month.
September at Kodaikanal: 12-22°C, monsoon retreating on the 2,133m hills. Solid window — Coaker's Walk views clearing, lake boating runs, peak crowds past.
September at Kotagiri: 13-23°C, oldest Nilgiri station at its quietest with misty tea slopes. Works well — clearer windows arrive week by week.
September at Munnar: 14-24°C, tea estates lush as monsoon retreats. Solid window — crowds thinner than Dec-Jan, clearer days arriving.
September at Nelliyampathy: 14-24°C, quiet plantations greening post-monsoon. Works well — zero infrastructure, book stays direct with estates.
September at Ooty: 12-22°C, Queen of the Nilgiris emerging from monsoon haze. Solid window — UNESCO toy train running, crowds thinner than peak summer.
September at Ponmudi: 18-26°C, 22 hairpin drives open as monsoon eases. Works well — butterfly sightings steady, Trivandrum day-trip friendly.
September at Thekkady: 18-26°C, Periyar reservoir full and spice plantations lush. Solid wildlife window — boat safaris run, elephant sightings improving.
September at Vagamon: 16-26°C, pine forests and meadows at their greenest. Works well — paragliding windows opening as weather clears mid-month.
September at Wayanad: 18-28°C, Western Ghats lush as monsoon retreats. Solid window — cave access returning, heart-shaped lake full, crowds light.
September at Yercaud: 16-26°C, Eastern Ghats hill station quieter than Nilgiri cousins. Works well — coffee estates lush, orange groves heading into fruit.
December at Ambaji: 10-24°C, crisp hilltop air and sharpest darshan window. Solid Shakti Peeth conditions — peak winter clarity at 480m.
December at Chinnar: 16-28°C, driest sanctuary months and firm trails. Works well — rain-shadow forest accessible, Thoovanam flow modest.
December at Coimbatore: 18-29°C, driest and coolest window of the year. Solid gateway to Nilgiris — Adiyogi walks comfortable at this elevation.
December at Coonoor: 8-20°C, dry tea slopes and clear Nilgiri viewpoints. Works well — quieter than Ooty, crisp winter at 1,850m.
December at Courtallam: 20-28°C, falls flow steady post-retreating monsoon. Solid bathing window — Aadi season (Jul-Aug) remains the cultural peak.
December at Dakor: 10-26°C, cool Krishna-temple mornings and dry darshan days. Works well — pleasant window ahead of Makar Sankranti footfall.
December at Eravikulam: 8-20°C, post-monsoon clarity on tahr slopes. Solid window — trails firm, visibility returning after September reopening.
December at Khandala: 12-26°C, dry Western Ghats viewpoints and clear Duke's Nose vistas. Works well — shoulder season, crowds thinner than monsoon peak.
December at Kodaikanal: 8-20°C, dry star-lake days and clear Coaker's Walk views. Solid winter window — weekends book out, crisp and accessible.
December at Kotagiri: 10-22°C, dry oldest-Nilgiri station and quiet tea estates. Works well — clearer views than Ooty nearby, lower elevation comfort.
December at Meghamalai: 10-22°C, dry cloud-forest days and clear viewpoints. Solid window — estate booking advance-only, zero tourism keeps it calm.
December at Mollem: 18-30°C, dry Western Ghats sanctuary and Dudhsagar approach drying out. Works well — Kadamba temple visits pleasant, wildlife trails firm.
December at Ooty: 6-19°C, dry botanical-garden days and clear Doddabetta views. Solid winter window — toy train running, weekends book out early.
December at Saputara: 8-24°C, dry Gujarat hill-station mornings and clear Western Ghats views. Works well — quieter than monsoon-festival peak.
December at Silent Valley: 14-26°C, dry rainforest trails and clear canopy views. Solid window — permits steady, wildlife visibility improving through winter.
December at Thrissur: 22-32°C, dry temple-town days and comfortable cultural-capital walks. Works well — pre-Pooram lull, April remains the showpiece.
December at Valparai: 12-22°C, dry hairpin drives and clear macaque-forest views. Solid window — shoulder to peak, tea-estate visibility sharp.
December at Yercaud: 14-24°C, dry Eastern Ghats hill-station days and clear coffee-estate walks. Works well — budget-friendly weekend window, crowds manageable.
